nodes.name: env.ts
nodes.summary: Loads and validates server/client environment variables for the auth service using t3-env and zod, including OAuth, Stripe, Redis, and QStash secrets.

nodes.name: accept-invitation-endpoint.ts
nodes.summary: Better Auth plugin exposing a POST /accept-invitation endpoint that verifies the invite token, creates or reuses the invited user, adds them to the organization inside a row-locked transaction, and establishes a new session.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a SELECT ... FOR UPDATE lock inside a Drizzle transaction to prevent a double-accept race on the invitation row.

nodes.name: generate-magic-token.ts
nodes.summary: Generates a cryptographically secure one-time token for organization invitations and stores it in the verifications table with a one-week expiry.

nodes.name: rate-limit.ts
nodes.summary: Configures an Upstash Redis-backed sliding-window rate limiter capping organization invitations to 10 per hour per inviter.

nodes.name: resend.ts
nodes.summary: Instantiates the shared Resend email client used to send transactional auth and billing emails.

nodes.name: resolve-session-organization-state.ts
nodes.summary: Resolves a session's active organization from the user's memberships and persists a corrected active-org id when it has gone stale, exposing injectable dependencies for testing.
nodes.languageNotes: Guards against a lost-update race by re-reading the persisted active organization when a concurrent session update wins the write.

nodes.name: seed-dev.ts
nodes.summary: Development-only script that creates or upgrades a local dev account, marking it onboarded and ensuring it has an active pro subscription.

nodes.name: server.ts
nodes.summary: Central Better Auth configuration bootstrapping the auth server: registers API-key, JWT, OAuth-provider, Expo, organization, bearer, custom-session, Stripe billing, and invitation-acceptance plugins, and wires lifecycle hooks for auto-enrollment, team membership, billing seat sync, Slack notifications, and transactional emails.
nodes.languageNotes: Aggregates a large number of Better Auth plugin configurations and lifecycle hooks (databaseHooks, organizationHooks, Stripe hooks) into a single exported `auth` instance.

nodes.name: stripe.ts
nodes.summary: Instantiates the shared Stripe SDK client used for billing operations across the auth service.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel file re-exporting billing utility functions (formatPrice, getOrganizationOwners) for use elsewhere in the auth package.

nodes.name: verificationMatchesInvitation
nodes.summary: Checks whether a verification token's identifier matches an invitation, either by exact invitation id or a case-insensitive email match.

nodes.name: getInvitationAcceptError
nodes.summary: Maps an error thrown while accepting an invitation (e.g. hitting the free-plan seat limit) to a user-facing message and HTTP status code.

nodes.name: generateMagicTokenForInvite
nodes.summary: Generates a 64-character hex token, persists it to the verifications table keyed to the invitation id, and returns it for inclusion in the invite link.

nodes.name: resolveSessionOrganizationState
nodes.summary: Computes the correct active organization for a session from the user's memberships, updates the session row when it is stale, and re-reads the persisted value if a concurrent update wins the race.

nodes.name: seedDevAccount
nodes.summary: Creates the local dev account if missing, marks it onboarded, and ensures its organization has an active pro subscription; throws outside NODE_ENV=development.

nodes.name: serializeCancellationDetails
nodes.summary: Safely extracts the comment, feedback, and reason fields from a Stripe subscription's cancellation details for inclusion in a notification payload, swallowing serialization errors.

nodes.name: mintUserJwt
nodes.summary: Mints a short-lived RS256 JWT via Better Auth's JWT plugin so headless services (e.g. the automations dispatcher) can act on behalf of a user without holding their session cookie.
